description:
You are acting as a release-manager who must give a quick green-light for today’s staging deployment.

    The repository already contains two JSON files:

    1. /home/user/release/env_schema.json  
    2. /home/user/release/env_descriptor.json  

    Your job is to:

    1. Validate env_descriptor.json against env_schema.json.  
       • If the descriptor conforms to the schema, append the single line  
         “STATUS: VALID” (without the quotes) to a log file called  
         /home/user/release/validation.log.  
       • If the descriptor does NOT conform, append the single line  
         “STATUS: INVALID” instead.  
       • Do not write anything else to validation.log.  
       • The file must end with a newline.

    2. Produce an easy-to-scan CSV summary of the container images involved in the release.  
       • Extract, in order of appearance, every element of .services[] from env_descriptor.json.  
       • For each element output:  
         service_name,image_reference  
         (comma-separated, no extra spaces).  
       • Save the result to /home/user/release/release_summary.csv, ending with a newline.

    3. Display (cat) both validation.log and release_summary.csv to the console so that a human can immediately read the results.

    Notes & constraints:
    • Use only standard Linux command-line tooling such as jq, python3 –m jsonschema (or any other package that is pre-installed in the container), grep, echo, etc.  
    • Do not modify the original env_schema.json or env_descriptor.json.  
    • The log and CSV must be created with filesystem permissions 0644 or more permissive (they just need to be readable).  
    • No additional files may be created in /home/user/release except the two specified outputs.  
    • The automated grader will check:  
      – The presence and exact one-line content of /home/user/release/validation.log.  
      – The existence and exact ordering/content of /home/user/release/release_summary.csv.  
      – That the files end with a single trailing newline and have no extra blank lines or spaces.  
      – That you printed both files’ contents to stdout as your final console output.
